# TDPdf v1.0.0 — Renamed from KillerPDF

This is the first release under the new name **TDPdf**, maintained by **The Doodle Project, LLC** (Frisco, TX). It is the same codebase previously released as `doodlemania2/KillerPDF` v0.9, renamed in a single atomic commit, with upstream personal branding removed as required by GPLv3 §7(e) and a new `NOTICE` file added under §5(a).

## A love letter to upstream

This project only exists because [**SteveTheKiller/KillerPDF**](https://github.com/SteveTheKiller/KillerPDF) is a *genuinely* great piece of software. Steve took on something nobody else seems willing to do — write a tasteful, fast, **dark-themed, no-telemetry, no-cloud, no-subscription** PDF viewer for Windows — and shipped it as a real WPF app you can just *run*. The visual design is sharp, the launcher dialog is delightful, the rendering is crisp, and the codebase is approachable enough that volunteers can actually contribute. None of the work here would exist without that foundation. **Go star the upstream project.** Seriously. Steve's original is the reason this fork is even worth doing.

## What this fork is

A **modernization + feature laboratory** sitting downstream of upstream. The goal isn't to compete with `SteveTheKiller/KillerPDF` — it's to push experimental changes through CI, prove them out, and (where they make sense) feed them back upstream. The fork tracks upstream commits and selectively pulls in any non-conflicting fixes.

## What changed in 1.0.0

### Rename (this release)

- Product, package, namespace, EXE, install directory, ProgID, ARP entry, registry keys, and uninstall batch all migrate to `TDPdf` / `TDPdf.pdf`.
- Publisher metadata in Add/Remove Programs now reads **The Doodle Project**.
- Wordmark, dialog titles, and crash-report header updated to TDPdf.
- Upstream personal branding ("Steve the Killer", `thekiller.net`, `killertools.net`) removed from UI, landing page, footer, and installer dialogs per GPLv3 §7(e).
- `NOTICE` file added at the repo root with the §5(a) modification notice.
- Application icon replaced with a placeholder TD glyph (`Resources/tdpdf-icon.ico`).

Versioning resets to **1.0.0** under the new identity. Existing KillerPDF installations are unaffected; TDPdf installs alongside them under its own install path and registry keys.

### Platform & build (inherited from v0.9 fork engineering)

- **.NET 9 (`net9.0-windows`)**, SDK-style csproj, nullable + implicit usings enabled.
- **Single-file self-contained publish** with native pdfium.dll bundled (`IncludeNativeLibrariesForSelfExtract`); Costura/Fody removed.
- **CI on an in-cluster ARC runner (`tdpdf`)** with `EnableWindowsTargeting=true` for Linux cross-build of net9.0-windows.
- **Required-status CodeQL** ruleset on `main` — every change is statically analyzed before merge.

### Architecture & code quality

- **MVVM foundation** via `CommunityToolkit.Mvvm` + a `MainWindowViewModel` scaffold for incremental migration of `MainWindow.xaml.cs`.
- **Service extraction** — dedicated `Services/` for PDF I/O, printing, cropping, and direct content editing; `ViewModels/` for zoom; `Models/` for annotations.

### Reliability

- **Global crash handler** wiring `DispatcherUnhandledException` + `AppDomain.UnhandledException` + `TaskScheduler.UnobservedTaskException`, with on-disk crash logs, recoverable-vs-fatal classification, themed crash dialog, and a status-message ring buffer for context.
- **Hardened release signing** in `release.ps1` — verify-on-update, time-stamp fallback, pdfium.dll integrity checks, encrypted-PDF temp hygiene.

### Performance & rendering

- **Async PDF I/O** — open, render, and thumbnails all run off the UI thread via `PdfDocumentService` with full `CancellationToken` support.
- **Per-monitor DPI awareness** — `PerMonitorV2` manifest + `WM_DPICHANGED` handling + DPI-aware page rendering keyed by `(pageIndex, dpiX)` render cache.
- **Virtualized sidebar** (`VirtualizingStackPanel` + recycling) and frozen brushes/bitmaps throughout the rendering pipeline.

### Features

- **Crop PDF pages** — single or all-pages, via `PdfRectangle.CropBox` (`Services/CropService.cs`).
- **Zoom controls** — +/− buttons, Fit-to-Width / Fit-to-Page, cursor-anchored wheel zoom, keyboard shortcuts (`ViewModels/ZoomViewModel.cs`).
- **Direct content editing** of existing PDF text and images (`Services/PdfContentEditor.cs`).
- **XPS print flow** — `PrintDialog` + `XpsDocumentWriter.WriteAsync` with page-range, copies, and progress reporting (`Services/PrintService.cs`).
- **Themes** — Dark (default), Light, High-Contrast, and follow-system; plus an optional **native Windows window frame** instead of the custom dark chrome.

### Accessibility

- `AutomationProperties` names on toolbar / sidebar / page list, focus visuals, contrast audit, full keyboard navigation.

### Windows integration

- File-association hardening: ProgID hygiene, `SHChangeNotify` post-install nudge, `UserChoice` detection, single-instance handling, robust CLI argument parsing.
- **Suppressed PCA popup** via Win10/11 `supportedOS` GUIDs in `app.manifest`.
- Skip "set as default" prompt when already default; themed default-app dialog (cherry-picked from upstream v1.3.2).

## Install

Download `TDPdf-v1.0.0-win-x64.exe` from this release. It's a self-contained single-file executable — no .NET runtime install required. First-launch dialog will offer to install into `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Programs\TDPdf\` and (optionally) set itself as the default PDF handler.

## Credits

- **Upstream**: [SteveTheKiller/KillerPDF](https://github.com/SteveTheKiller/KillerPDF) — the entire reason any of this exists.
- **Fork**: [doodlemania2/TDPdf](https://github.com/doodlemania2/TDPdf), maintained by The Doodle Project, LLC.
